reasoning: The market asks if Donald Trump will initiate legal action against South Park by Dec 31, 2025, resulting in negative repercussions (penalties, restrictions, revenue/subscriber loss). While the hypothetical/actual timeline places us in late July 2025 with Trump in the White House responding negatively to the show, achieving formal legal action that *causes* one of the specified negative outcomes within roughly 5 months is a high hurdle. C&Ds rarely cause direct material damage to major networks, lawsuits take years and are usually dismissed via Anti-SLAPP before imposing penalties (and often result in net-positive publicity/revenue), and regulatory actions (like FCC pressure) are slow and not fully applicable to cable networks like Comedy Central. Thus, despite high tensions, a strict 'Yes' resolution is unlikely in such a short window. I am forecasting slightly below the 22.6% market consensus, given the rigid requirements for the action itself to directly cause negative repercussions.
